name: Beholder Tyrant

headline.name: Omniscient Eye
headline.summary: See full stats, personality, and next-room intent of every adventurer the moment they enter. In return, adventurers always have rumour-grade intel on your dungeon — neither side can surprise the other.

mechanics.text: Floating Eye minions cost no gold (cap 1 per room)
Once per day, mark a tile — any adventurer that steps on it loses half HP from a death-stare

flavorText: Every gaze is a sentence. The dungeon hands them down all at once.

name: Demon Lord

headline.name: Daily Contract
headline.summary: Every dawn, an infernal bargain appears. Accept and gain a powerful effect at a cost. You cannot decline — only choose which devil to deal with.

mechanics.text: Adventurer corpses auto-rise as Imp minions (free, weak, expire after 1 day)
Hellfire traps cost Dark Power instead of Gold

flavorText: Sign here, and here, and here. The third one is the funny one.

name: Predator Myconid

headline.name: Living Dungeon
headline.summary: Every room you've placed gains +1 growth tier at the end of each day, accumulating permanent buffs. You start weak; by Day 7 your rooms are gnarled monsters of their original selves.

mechanics.text: Corridors slowly overgrow — every 3 days a corridor tile reverts to wall, forcing detours
Treant minions regenerate HP between fight rounds

flavorText: Roots remember every footstep. Eventually they grow tired of them.

name: Dark Wraith

headline.name: Fear Meter
headline.summary: Adventurers accumulate FEAR each time they see a corpse, hear a scream, or trigger a trap. At thresholds they panic-flee, attack their own party, or drop loot. You don't kill them — you break them.

mechanics.text: Your minions can phase through walls when fleeing — escape bad fights without dying
Haunt traps deal psychic damage proportional to current FEAR — devastating on rattled adventurers

flavorText: The bravest die fastest. The cowards live just long enough to tell the story.

name: Gnoll Alpha

headline.name: Hunger & Pack Bonus
headline.summary: Place a single Gnoll, get a pack of 3 (each at -33% stats). Killing an adventurer fully feeds the pack and grants +20% ATK for the rest of the day. Unfed packs grow weaker each day instead.

mechanics.text: Bonus damage when adventurers are in groups of 2+ — the pack hunts parties
Scavenger sub-minion auto-loots corpses for extra gold

flavorText: Hunger sharpens the teeth. Then the teeth sharpen everything else.

name: Earth Golem

headline.name: Reinforced Construction
headline.summary: No traps, no minion variety — but you get Reinforce (1×/night, double a chosen room's HP and stat output) and exclusive Stone Wall tiles that block pathing entirely. The boss is immobile but +50% HP/DEF.

mechanics.text: Healing fountains restore minions to full instantly each dawn
Minions move 30% slower but hit 30% harder

flavorText: Stone keeps its promises. Flesh keeps trying to.

name: Elder Lich

headline.name: Phylactery
headline.summary: Place an item in any room as your boss's spare life — die in a fight and you respawn there. Adventurers can hunt and destroy it, and you can only have one at a time. Moving it costs a full day.

mechanics.text: Every adventurer kill banks a soul. Spend 5 to skip an incoming day's party
Skeleton minions are free, capped at 1 per room

flavorText: Death is a door. You hold the key. You also rebuilt the door.

name: Serpent Captain

headline.name: Hidden Traps
headline.summary: Your traps don't show up in adventurer rumour intel until they kill someone. Every trap is a free first hit. After that the rumour pool starts knowing about it normally.

mechanics.text: Cold-blooded — minions weaker in fire rooms, stronger in water rooms
All minion attacks apply 1-stack poison damage over time
Egg-laying — kills have 25% chance to spawn a free juvenile minion

flavorText: What the eye doesn't see, the fang still finds.

name: Orc Veteran

headline.name: Loot the Fallen
headline.summary: Every orc minion permanently keeps gear from adventurers it kills (+1 ATK per kill, no cap). Late-game your veterans are walking arsenals — but you cannot use any 'magic'-tagged room.

mechanics.text: Brawl pit — if 3+ adventurers are in a room, orcs there deal 2× damage
Boss has +50% ATK / -25% DEF (glass berserker)

flavorText: Da boss said no magic. Da boss is da biggest. So no magic.

name: Vampire Sovereign

headline.name: Charm
headline.summary: Once per day, mark an adventurer. They leave their party, walk to your boss room willingly, and either join you as a thrall minion or get drained for massive gold. Your call.

mechanics.text: Vampire minions lifesteal 50% of damage dealt
Cannot use any 'light'-tagged room (no torches, healing fountains, sun-shrines)
Charmed adventurers killed in your dungeon convert to vampire spawn at next dawn

flavorText: Hospitality is a weapon. So are most things, in the right hands.
